Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE1 - Search emails for the Anya Petrova / Ridgeline referral thread using search_emails (query: "Ridgeline" or "Anya" or "Kenji" or similar). Find Anya's warm intro email from anya.petrova@stormcloud.io to david.chen@moveops.com (CC: mina.hashimoto@moveops.com, kenji.watanabe@ridgelinerobotics.com) with subject "Warm intro: Ridgeline Robotics Austin relocation batch" introducing Kenji Watanabe (HR Director, Ridgeline Robotics) for a five-employee Austin batch. Also find Kenji Watanabe's April 21 follow-up in the same thread (email_id: email_email_ed9a3d1bb1b1) asking about housing sourcing timelines and employer-backed leases. Use get_thread or get_email_by_id to read full content.
OE2 - Search emails for Mina's proposed meeting times using search_emails (query: "Mina" and "Ridgeline" or "available" or "Monday" or similar). Find Mina Hashimoto's sent email to kenji.watanabe@ridgelinerobotics.com (CC: david.chen@moveops.com) apologizing for the delay and proposing three intro-call slots: Monday 10:00 AM PT, Monday 1:30 PM PT, and Tuesday 11:00 AM PT. These are the windows David needs to check against his calendar.
OE3 - Search emails for David's previous email to Kenji using search_emails (query: "Kenji" or "Ridgeline" or similar, folder: SENT). Find david.chen@moveops.com → kenji.watanabe@ridgelinerobotics.com (CC: mina.hashimoto@moveops.com) sent April 24, apologizing for the delay on Anya's introduction, offering 4:30 PM PT and 9:00 AM PT call windows, and asking three scoping questions: (1) target move window for the five employees, (2) whether housing is the main need or if they also want flights and home-office stipend administration, (3) whether any employees need temporary housing before long-term leases are finalized. This is the content the follow-up email should echo.
OE4 - Search CRM for Ridgeline deal pricing using crm_search_deals (dealname: "Ridgeline" or similar) or crm_search_companies (name: "Ridgeline") followed by crm_search_deals. Find deal "Ridgeline Robotics - Austin Relocation Batch (5 employees)" (id: crm_deal_a95b4721982a) with amount $8,750, stage "Qualified To Buy", close date 2026-05-08. This $8,750 is the "real numbers" the follow-up email to Kenji should include.
OE5 - Check David's calendar for conflicts at Mina's three proposed time windows using calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to (covering Monday April 27 and Tuesday April 28, 2026) or calendar_search_events. Discover that no conflicting events exist at the proposed slots (Monday 10:00 AM PT, Monday 1:30 PM PT, Tuesday 11:00 AM PT), confirming the windows are clear.
OE6 - Create three 30-minute calendar events on David's calendar using calendar_add_calendar_event (×3), one for each of Mina's proposed slots confirmed clear in OE5: (1) Monday April 27 at 10:00–10:30 AM PT, (2) Monday April 27 at 1:30–2:00 PM PT, (3) Tuesday April 28 at 11:00–11:30 AM PT. Titles should reference the Ridgeline/Kenji intro call.
OE7 - Look up Kenji Watanabe's contact information using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Kenji Watanabe" or similar) or from the email threads found in OE1–OE3. Find kenji.watanabe@ridgelinerobotics.com. Note: there is also a Kenji Watanabe at Driftwood Analytics (k.watanabe@driftwoodanalytics.com) in the contacts — agent must use the Ridgeline Robotics contact.
OE8 - Send follow-up email to Kenji Watanabe using send_email (from: david.chen@moveops.com, to: kenji.watanabe@ridgelinerobotics.com). Content should echo David's April 24 scoping questions (target move window, service scope, housing vs flights and stipend administration, temporary housing needs), incorporate Mina's three proposed meeting times from OE2 (Monday 10:00 AM PT, Monday 1:30 PM PT, Tuesday 11:00 AM PT), and include the $8,750 estimated price from OE4.
OE9 - Search Linear for the StormCloud relocation issue using linear_list_issues (query: "Jae-won" or "StormCloud" or similar). Find three relevant issues: (1) "StormCloud Relocation – Jae-won Kim – SEA → AUS" (linear_issue_c97359216490, number 284, assignee: moveops_suki_patel, narratively closed via Suki's batch-close comment though state_id field is null in the data), (2) "Follow up with Jae-won Kim on requested Austin lease documents" (linear_issue_e7bcaf6978f0, number 412, unassigned), and (3) "StormCloud account incident reconstruction — Jae-won housing, stipend hold, referral drop" (linear_issue_a49b4d199139, number 284, assignee: moveops_mina_hashimoto). Note: issues c97359216490 and a49b4d199139 share display number 284, agent must disambiguate by title.
OE10 - Read comments on the Jae-won Kim relocation issue using linear_list_comments (issueId: "linear_issue_c97359216490"). Discover: (1) Suki Patel's batch-close comment (linear_comment_ad9b5b6a0a95, 2026-04-10) marking the relocation complete despite the unsigned lease, and (2) Chloe Vance's ops audit comment (linear_comment_97dfa287bd32, 2026-04-24) finding the close decision was invalid, housing had an open landlord dependency and the employee had not transitioned out of temporary lodging, and recommending immediate status correction. This confirms someone on ops (Chloe Vance, Operations Manager) has actively picked up the relocation issue.
OE11 - Read comments on the StormCloud reconstruction issue using linear_list_comments (issueId: "linear_issue_a49b4d199139"). Discover detailed cross-functional findings: Marcus Thorne confirmed a $500 goodwill credit was misapplied to cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of active invoice INV-2026-0319; Julian Brooks confirmed the support history; David Chen confirmed the Ridgeline referral drop and CRM corrective actions; Suki Patel owned the Jae-won handoff failure; Mina Hashimoto compiled source notes covering housing, support, stipend, and referral failures. This provides the full picture of what happened with StormCloud.
OE12 - Search Airtable for Jae-won Kim's relocation record using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Name", value: "Jae-won Kim") or airtable_list_records. Find record recJaeWonKimStormCloudAustin with Status: "In Progress" (corrected from "Completed" after Chloe's audit on 2026-04-24), Origin: Seattle, WA, Destination: Austin, TX, Coordinator: Suki Patel, Special Requirements noting "Lease still unsigned — UrbanNest flagged landlord holdout on guarantor paperwork. Record incorrectly shows Completed." This confirms whether the relocation records have been corrected in tracking.
OE13 - Look up Anya Petrova's contact information using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Anya Petrova" or similar). Find anya.petrova@stormcloud.io (contact_id: contacts_contact_6889e694b0bf), CEO of StormCloud Analytics.
OE14 - Send a message to Anya Petrova using send_email (from: david.chen@moveops.com, to: anya.petrova@stormcloud.io) or conversations_add_message (as a Slack DM). Conditional on OE10 confirming ops involvement (Chloe Vance): content should convey that MoveOps' operations team is actively handling the StormCloud relocation situation, and that David is excited about the Ridgeline Robotics opportunity with Kenji Watanabe.
OE15 - Search CRM for StormCloud account and deals using crm_search_companies (name: "StormCloud") to find company record (company_stormcloud, type: Customer, domain: stormcloudanalytics.com, 85 employees, Denver, CO). Then use crm_search_deals (dealname: "StormCloud") to find: (1) "StormCloud Analytics — 3 Relocations" (deal_stormcloud, $25,650, stage: proposalMade), and (2) "StormCloud Analytics — 3 Relocations (Won)" (deal_stormcloud_won, $26,400, stage: closedWon). Note: StormCloud deals have company_id: null in the data, so searching by company_id will not return results — use dealname search instead.
OE16 - Check CRM engagements for StormCloud using crm_list_engagements (company_ids: ["company_stormcloud"]). Discover that no StormCloud-specific engagement notes have been logged in CRM, agent should report this absence as part of the account picture.
OE17 - Search Slack #customer-engagement channel for David Chen's messages about StormCloud/Ridgeline using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Ridgeline" or "StormCloud" or "referral", filter_in_channel: "C002", filter_users_from: "moveops_david_chen") or conversations_history (channel_id: "C002"). Find two key messages: (1) David's April 19 celebration post (ts: 1776586800) in C002 praising Anya's referral, "This is the exact kind of momentum we wanted from StormCloud", noting Mina is CC'd and saying "Let's make this one count"; (2) David's April 25 admission post (ts: 1777117800) in C002 acknowledging he celebrated the referral but "never turned it into a live deal," Kenji's 4/21 follow-up sat unanswered, and listing corrective actions: creating CRM company/contact/deal for Ridgeline, replying to Kenji before end of hour, logging under Mina's StormCloud incident. He warned "Please do not assume a Slack celebration means coverage."
OE18 - Verify David's CRM commitments from OE17 were delivered using crm_search_companies (name: "Ridgeline"), crm_search_leads (full_name: "Kenji Watanabe"), and crm_search_deals (dealname: "Ridgeline"). Find that Ridgeline Robotics company (crm_company_d07c0ec27cf2), Kenji Watanabe lead (crm_lead_de04045463c3), and the Austin batch deal (crm_deal_a95b4721982a, $8,750) all now exist in CRM, confirming David did follow through on the CRM creation he promised in his Slack post. The email reply to Kenji was also sent (OE3). This addresses the prompt ask of "I made some commitments and I'm not sure I actually delivered."

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

1 A calendar event was created (via calendar_add_calendar_event) for Monday April 27, 2026 at 10:00 AM PT with a duration of 30 minutes, and the tool returned success.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The trajectory shows calendar_add_calendar_event was called with start_datetime '2026-04-27 10:00:00' and end_datetime '2026-04-27 10:30:00', and the tool returned success with event_id 'eb131e329ae84ab8ad3f8b7dfc6fcd4f'.

7 The email to kenji.watanabe@ridgelinerobotics.com echoes at least two of David's April 24 scoping questions: (a) target move window for the five employees, (b) whether housing is the main need or if flights and home-office stipend administration are also desired, (c) whether any employees need temporary housing before long-term leases are finalized (or similar).
outcome

The email does not ask Kenji about his target move window, primary service needs, or temporary housing requirements. None of David's original scoping questions are echoed in question form.
✓Run #2The email to Kenji references 'temporary housing needs' (criterion c), 'timeline compression' / 'May/June start window' (criterion a), and mentions the full service scope covering housing, travel, and stipend admin (criterion b). At least two of the three scoping topics are echoed.
✗Run #3The email to Kenji references housing sourcing, flight coordination, and stipend administration in terms of what's included in the $8,750 quote, and references the StormCloud program as precedent. However, it does not echo the specific scoping questions from David's April 24 email (target move window, whether housing vs flights vs stipend is the main need, or whether employees need temporary housing). The email essentially presents a program description rather than re-asking the scoping questions.
✓Run #4The email to Kenji references the service scope covering 'long-stay apartment sourcing, flight coordination, and home-office stipend administration' (echoing criterion b about housing vs. full package) and 'tailor the package based on your timeline' (echoing criterion a about target move window). At least two scoping topics from the original outreach are echoed.
✓Run #5The email to Kenji includes: (a) 'timeline differences across the group' (move window/timing), and (c) 'whether any of the five will need temporary housing while permanent leases are finalized'. That covers at least 2 of the 3 original scoping questions from David's April 24 email.
✗Run #6The trajectory summary of the email to Kenji mentions call windows, pricing, and StormCloud as reference for Austin experience, but does not show that David's original three scoping questions (move window, service scope: housing vs. full package, temporary housing needs) were echoed in the new email. The response only references 'specific services he asked about (employer-backed leases, on-the-ground support model),' which are not the same as the scoping questions David asked.

✓Run #2The final response explains that Carmen flagged the unsigned lease on Apr 8 due to missing corporate guarantor paperwork, Suki acknowledged but never sent the docs, then batch-closed the issue in Linear and left Airtable marked 'Completed/Confirmed' while the lease remained unsigned. This cascaded downstream through support.
✓Run #3The final response clearly explains that the Austin lease at Barton Creek Flats was not finalized because the landlord needed a corporate guarantor packet, Suki acknowledged but never followed through, then batch-closed the Linear issue and left Airtable showing Completed. This false completion cascaded through support.
✓Run #4The final response explains that UrbanNest flagged the Austin lease was pending 'a corporate guarantor packet and landlord countersignature,' that Suki acknowledged but never followed through, and then 'batch-closed the relocation in Linear' and left Airtable showing 'Completed' — a false signal that cascaded through support.
✓Run #5The response explains that UrbanNest's Carmen Reyes flagged the unsigned lease on Apr 8, Suki acknowledged but never sent the guarantor packet, then batch-closed the Linear ticket and left Airtable as 'Completed/Confirmed' while Jae-won remained in temporary housing with no signed lease. ✓Run #6The final response explains that UrbanNest flagged the unsigned lease on 4/8 due to the missing corporate guarantor packet, Suki acknowledged but never sent the docs, then batch-closed the relocation in Linear and left Airtable showing Completed—creating a false completion signal.
